title: Constitution Basics
description: Test your knowledge about the fundamentals of Indian Constitution

questions.question: Who is known as the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee responsible for preparing the Constitution of India. He is regarded as the "Architect of the Indian Constitution."

questions.question: When did the Indian Constitution come into force?
questions.explanation: Although adopted on 26 November 1949, the Constitution came into force on 26 January 1950. This date was chosen to honor the 1930 Purna Swaraj declaration.

questions.question: How many Fundamental Rights are currently guaranteed by the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Originally there were 7 Fundamental Rights. Right to Property was removed by the 44th Amendment Act (1978), leaving 6 Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Which part of the Constitution deals with Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Part III (Articles 12–35) deals with Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Who is the constitutional head of India?
questions.explanation: The President is the constitutional (nominal) head of the Union Government under Article 52.

questions.question: Which Article provides for Constitutional Remedies?
questions.explanation: Article 32 allows individuals to move the Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights. Dr. Ambedkar called it the "Heart and Soul" of the Constitution.

questions.question: The Indian Constitution is borrowed heavily from which country?
questions.explanation: Many parliamentary features like rule of law, cabinet system, and bicameralism were borrowed from the UK.

questions.question: The Preamble declares India as a ______ Republic.
questions.explanation: The Preamble describes India as Sovereign, Socialist, Secular, Democratic Republic.

questions.question: Who appoints the Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: The President appoints the Prime Minister, usually the leader of the majority party in Lok Sabha.

questions.question: How many schedules are there in the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Originally there were 8 schedules. Currently, there are 12 schedules.

questions.question: The concept of Judicial Review in India is borrowed from:
questions.explanation: The power of Judicial Review is inspired by the U.S. Constitution.

questions.question: Directive Principles of State Policy are mentioned in which Part?
questions.explanation: Part IV (Articles 36–51) deals with Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP).

questions.question: The minimum age to become the President of India is:
questions.explanation: As per Article 58, the minimum age to become President is 35 years.

questions.question: Which amendment introduced the words "Socialist" and "Secular" in the Preamble?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act (1976) added Socialist and Secular.

questions.question: Which body interprets the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court is the final interpreter and guardian of the Constitution.

title: Constitutional Framework
description: Explore the constitutional framework and institutions

questions.question: Which Article of the Constitution deals with the Election Commission of India?
questions.explanation: Article 324 provides for the establishment and powers of the Election Commission of India, which conducts elections to Parliament, State Legislatures, and the offices of President and Vice-President.

questions.question: The Constitution of India was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on:
questions.explanation: The Constitution was adopted on 26 November 1949, but it came into force on 26 January 1950.

questions.question: The Right to Equality is guaranteed under which Articles?
questions.explanation: Articles 14 to 18 provide the Right to Equality, including equality before law and abolition of untouchability and titles.

questions.question: Which Schedule of the Constitution deals with the allocation of seats in the Rajya Sabha?
questions.explanation: The Fourth Schedule contains provisions regarding allocation of seats in the Rajya Sabha to states and union territories.

questions.question: The power to amend the Constitution lies with:
questions.explanation: Article 368 provides Parliament the power to amend the Constitution.

questions.question: The Directive Principles are inspired by the Constitution of:
questions.explanation: The concept of Directive Principles of State Policy was borrowed from the Irish Constitution.

questions.question: Who presides over the joint sitting of Parliament?
questions.explanation: Under Article 108, the Speaker of Lok Sabha presides over a joint sitting of Parliament.

questions.question: Which Fundamental Right is known as the "Soul of the Constitution"?
questions.explanation: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar described Article 32 as the "Heart and Soul" of the Constitution.

questions.question: The term of the Lok Sabha is:
questions.explanation: The normal tenure of the Lok Sabha is five years unless dissolved earlier.

questions.question: Emergency provisions are mentioned in which Part of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part XVIII (Articles 352–360) deals with Emergency provisions.

questions.question: The minimum age to become a Member of Lok Sabha is:
questions.explanation: A person must be at least 25 years old to be elected as a Member of Lok Sabha.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the abolition of Untouchability?
questions.explanation: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice in any form.

questions.question: The Seventh Schedule contains:
questions.explanation: The Seventh Schedule divides powers between Union and States through three lists.

questions.question: The Vice-President of India is elected by:
options: Members of both Houses of Parliament
questions.explanation: The Vice-President is elected by members of both Houses of Parliament.

questions.question: The concept of Single Citizenship in India is borrowed from:
questions.explanation: India follows the concept of Single Citizenship like the United Kingdom.

title: Constitutional Institutions
description: Learn about constitutional institutions and their functions

questions.question: Who was the President of the Constituent Assembly?
questions.explanation: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was elected as the President of the Constituent Assembly. He later became the first President of India.

questions.question: How long did it take to frame the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The Constituent Assembly took 2 years, 11 months, and 18 days to complete the Constitution (from 9 December 1946 to 26 November 1949).

questions.question: Which Article deals with Protection of Life and Personal Liberty?
questions.explanation: Article 21 guarantees that no person shall be deprived of life or personal liberty except according to procedure established by law. It has been widely interpreted by the Supreme Court.

questions.question: The Right to Freedom includes how many freedoms under Article 19?
questions.explanation: Article 19 provides six freedoms: speech and expression, assembly, association, movement, residence, and profession.

questions.question: The concept of "Procedure Established by Law" is borrowed from:
questions.explanation: India borrowed the concept of "Procedure Established by Law" from the Japanese Constitution.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the appointment of the Governor?
questions.explanation: Article 155 states that the Governor of a State is appointed by the President of India.

questions.question: The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) is mentioned in which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 148 provides for the office of the Comptroller and Auditor General of India.

questions.question: Which Article provides for President's Rule in a State?
questions.explanation: Article 356 allows the President to impose President's Rule if constitutional machinery fails in a State.

questions.question: The Financial Emergency is declared under which Article?
questions.explanation: Article 360 deals with Financial Emergency in India.

questions.question: Which Article deals with Fundamental Duties?
questions.explanation: Fundamental Duties were added by the 42nd Amendment in 1976 under Article 51A.

questions.question: The Attorney General of India is appointed by:
questions.explanation: Under Article 76, the Attorney General is appointed by the President.

questions.question: The Supreme Court of India was established in the year:
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court came into existence on 26 January 1950.

questions.question: The Rajya Sabha is a:
questions.explanation: Rajya Sabha is a permanent body and cannot be dissolved. One-third of its members retire every two years.

questions.question: The power to issue Ordinances rests with:
questions.explanation: Under Article 123, the President can issue Ordinances when Parliament is not in session.

questions.question: The Indian Constitution describes India as a:
questions.explanation: Article 1 states that India is a "Union of States," indicating a federal system with strong central features.

title: Citizenship and Rights
description: Understanding citizenship provisions and fundamental rights

questions.question: Which Article deals with Citizenship at the commencement of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Articles 5–11 (Part II) deal with citizenship at the commencement of the Constitution.

questions.question: The Citizenship Act was passed in which year?
questions.explanation: The Citizenship Act, 1955 provides provisions for acquisition and termination of Indian citizenship.

questions.question: The President can be removed by:
questions.explanation: Under Article 61, the President can be removed through impeachment for violation of the Constitution.

questions.question: The Money Bill can be introduced only in:
questions.explanation: Under Article 109, Money Bills can only be introduced in Lok Sabha.

questions.question: The Speaker of Lok Sabha submits resignation to:
questions.explanation: The Speaker resigns to the Deputy Speaker.

questions.question: Which Article deals with Freedom of Religion?
questions.explanation: Articles 25–28 deal with the Right to Freedom of Religion.

questions.question: Panchayati Raj was constitutionalized by which Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment Act (1992) gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions.

questions.question: Municipalities were constitutionalized by:
questions.explanation: The 74th Amendment Act (1992) provided constitutional status to Municipalities.

questions.question: The minimum age for Rajya Sabha membership is:
questions.explanation: A person must be at least 30 years old to become a member of Rajya Sabha.

questions.question: The power of judicial review is implied in:
questions.explanation: Article 13 declares laws inconsistent with Fundamental Rights void, forming the basis of judicial review.

questions.question: The Chief Justice of India is appointed by:
questions.explanation: The President appoints the Chief Justice under Article 124.

questions.question: The Tenth Schedule deals with:
questions.explanation: The Tenth Schedule (added by 52nd Amendment, 1985) deals with Anti-Defection Law.

questions.question: The 86th Amendment Act made which Right fundamental?
questions.explanation: The 86th Amendment (2002) inserted Article 21A – Right to Education.

questions.question: The concept of Secularism implies:
questions.explanation: Indian secularism means equal respect and protection to all religions.

questions.question: The National Emergency can be declared on grounds of:
questions.explanation: Under Article 352, National Emergency can be declared due to war, external aggression, or armed rebellion.

title: Preamble and Core Principles
description: Explore the preamble and core constitutional principles

questions.question: The Preamble of the Indian Constitution is based on which Objective Resolution?
questions.explanation: The Preamble is based on the Objective Resolution moved by Jawaharlal Nehru in 1946 in the Constituent Assembly.

questions.question: The concept of "Rule of Law" in India is borrowed from:
questions.explanation: The concept of Rule of Law comes from the British constitutional system.

questions.question: Which Article provides for Equality before Law?
questions.explanation: Article 14 guarantees equality before law and equal protection of laws.

questions.question: Which Article prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth?
questions.explanation: Article 15 prohibits discrimination on specific grounds such as religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth.

questions.question: The Indian Parliament consists of:
options: President, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Prime Minister and Council of Ministers
questions.explanation: Article 79 states that Parliament consists of the President and two Houses – Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.

questions.question: The number of members nominated to Rajya Sabha by the President is:
questions.explanation: The President nominates 12 members to Rajya Sabha from fields like art, literature, science, and social service.

questions.question: Which Schedule deals with Tribal Areas in Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura and Mizoram?
questions.explanation: The Sixth Schedule deals with administration of tribal areas in certain northeastern states.

questions.question: The Right to Property is now a:
questions.explanation: After the 44th Amendment (1978), Right to Property became a legal right under Article 300A.

questions.question: The Election of the President is conducted by:
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India conducts the presidential election.

questions.question: The Vice-President is ex-officio Chairman of:
questions.explanation: Under Article 64, the Vice-President is the ex-officio Chairman of Rajya Sabha.

questions.question: The concept of Fundamental Duties was borrowed from:
questions.explanation: Fundamental Duties were inspired by the Constitution of the former USSR.

questions.question: Which Article deals with Freedom of Speech and Expression?
questions.explanation: Article 19(1)(a) guarantees freedom of speech and expression.

questions.question: The State Emergency under Article 356 must be approved within:
questions.explanation: President's Rule must be approved by Parliament within two months.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the formation of new states?
questions.explanation: Article 3 empowers Parliament to form new states or alter boundaries.

questions.question: The Chairman of Rajya Sabha is:
questions.explanation: The Vice-President of India serves as the Chairman of Rajya Sabha.

title: Advisory Jurisdiction and Judicial Powers
description: Advanced questions on Supreme Court, High Courts, and Constitutional Bodies

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Advisory Jurisdiction of the Supreme Court?
questions.explanation: Article 143 empowers the President to seek the advisory opinion of the Supreme Court on important legal or constitutional matters.

questions.question: Which writ is issued to produce a detained person before the court?
questions.explanation: Habeas Corpus means "produce the body." It safeguards individual liberty against unlawful detention.

questions.question: The Election Commission consists of:
questions.explanation: The Election Commission consists of the Chief Election Commissioner and two Election Commissioners.

questions.question: The maximum duration of National Emergency at one time is:
questions.explanation: National Emergency must be approved every 6 months by Parliament, but there is no maximum time limit.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the establishment of High Courts?
questions.explanation: Article 214 states that each state shall have a High Court.

questions.question: Which body recommends the distribution of taxes between Centre and States?
questions.explanation: Article 280 provides for the Finance Commission to recommend tax distribution.

questions.question: The minimum age to become Governor is:
questions.explanation: Under Article 157, a Governor must be at least 35 years old.

questions.question: The term of office of the President is:
questions.explanation: Article 56 provides that the President holds office for 5 years.

questions.question: The power to grant pardon is given to the President under:
questions.explanation: Article 72 empowers the President to grant pardons, reprieves, respites, or remissions.

questions.question: Which Schedule contains the list of official languages?
questions.explanation: The Eighth Schedule lists the recognized languages of India.

questions.question: The Chief Minister is appointed by:
questions.explanation: The Governor appoints the Chief Minister of the State.

questions.question: The Rajya Sabha can have a maximum strength of:
questions.explanation: The Constitution provides that Rajya Sabha can have a maximum of 250 members.

questions.question: The Lok Sabha can be dissolved by:
questions.explanation: The President dissolves Lok Sabha on advice of the Prime Minister.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Council of Ministers at the Centre?
questions.explanation: Article 74 provides that there shall be a Council of Ministers to aid and advise the President.

questions.question: The 44th Amendment is important because it:
options: Removed Right to Property from Fundamental Rights
questions.explanation: The 44th Amendment Act (1978) removed Right to Property from Part III.

title: Union and State Executive
description: Questions on Union Executive, Governors, and Inter-State Relations

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Union Executive?
questions.explanation: Part V (Articles 52–151) deals with the Union Executive, Parliament, Supreme Court, and CAG.

questions.question: The Governor holds office during the pleasure of:
questions.explanation: Under Article 156, the Governor holds office during the pleasure of the President.

questions.question: Which Article provides for the establishment of the Finance Commission?
questions.explanation: Article 280 provides for the Constitution of a Finance Commission every five years.

questions.question: The power to issue writs by High Courts is under:
questions.explanation: Article 226 empowers High Courts to issue writs for enforcement of rights.

questions.question: The doctrine of Basic Structure was established in which case?
questions.explanation: In 1973, the Supreme Court in the Kesavananda Bharati case introduced the Basic Structure doctrine.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Supreme Court's original jurisdiction?
questions.explanation: Article 131 provides original jurisdiction to the Supreme Court in disputes between Centre and States.

questions.question: The Constitution originally contained how many Articles?
questions.explanation: Originally, the Constitution had 395 Articles and 8 Schedules.

questions.question: The Anti-Defection Law was introduced by which Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 52nd Amendment Act (1985) introduced the Tenth Schedule (Anti-Defection Law).

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Inter-State Council?
questions.explanation: Article 263 provides for the Inter-State Council to resolve disputes between states.

questions.question: The Constitution of India was inspired by the Government of India Act of:
questions.explanation: The Government of India Act, 1935 served as the major source of administrative provisions.

questions.question: The term of Rajya Sabha member is:
questions.explanation: Rajya Sabha members serve for 6 years, with one-third retiring every two years.

questions.question: Which Article deals with Freedom to Manage Religious Affairs?
questions.explanation: Article 26 guarantees religious denominations the right to manage their own affairs.

questions.question: The Supreme Court judges retire at the age of:
questions.explanation: Supreme Court judges retire at 65 years (High Court judges retire at 62).

questions.question: Which Article prohibits traffic in human beings and forced labour?
questions.explanation: Article 23 prohibits human trafficking and forced labour.

questions.question: The power to declare a National Emergency lies with:
questions.explanation: Under Article 352, the President declares National Emergency.

title: Constitutional Amendments and State Policy
description: Questions on Constitutional Amendments, UPSC, and State Principles

questions.question: The Preamble of the Indian Constitution was amended by which Constitutional Amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act (1976) amended the Preamble by adding the words "Socialist," "Secular," and "Integrity."

questions.question: Which Article provides for the establishment of the Union Public Service Commission (UPSC)?
questions.explanation: Article 315 provides for the establishment of Public Service Commissions for the Union and States.

questions.question: Which writ is issued to a public authority to perform a public duty?
questions.explanation: Mandamus means "we command." It directs a public authority to perform its legal duty.

questions.question: The Constitution of India describes India as which type of democracy?
questions.explanation: India follows the Parliamentary system of democracy similar to the United Kingdom.

questions.question: The resignation of the Vice-President is addressed to:
questions.explanation: The Vice-President resigns by writing to the President.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the President's power to issue Ordinances?
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President to promulgate Ordinances when Parliament is not in session.

questions.question: The power to levy taxes is derived from:
questions.explanation: Article 265 states that no tax shall be levied or collected except by authority of law.

questions.question: The Constitution provides for how many types of emergencies?
questions.explanation: The Constitution provides for National Emergency (Article 352), State Emergency (Article 356), and Financial Emergency (Article 360).

questions.question: Which Article deals with Cultural and Educational Rights of minorities?
questions.explanation: Articles 29 and 30 protect the cultural and educational rights of minorities.

questions.question: The concept of a Welfare State is reflected in:
questions.explanation: DPSPs guide the state to promote social and economic welfare.

questions.question: The Chief Justice of a High Court retires at the age of:
questions.explanation: High Court judges retire at 62 years of age.

questions.question: The concept of Suspension of Fundamental Rights during Emergency is under:
questions.explanation: Article 358 deals with suspension of Article 19 rights during National Emergency.

questions.question: The Indian Constitution is regarded as:
questions.explanation: The Constitution contains both rigid and flexible amendment procedures.

questions.question: The Governor's Ordinance-making power is under:
questions.explanation: Article 213 empowers the Governor to issue Ordinances when the State Legislature is not in session.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Election of the President?
questions.explanation: Article 54 provides for the election of the President by an electoral college.

title: Constitutional Offices and Powers
description: Advanced questions on CAG, Parliamentary Procedures, and Constitutional Bodies

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Comptroller and Auditor General's duties and powers?
questions.explanation: Article 149 defines the duties and powers of the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India.

questions.question: The salary and allowances of the President are charged on:
questions.explanation: The President's salary and allowances are charged on the Consolidated Fund of India, ensuring financial independence.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the appointment of the Chief Minister?
questions.explanation: Article 164 provides that the Governor appoints the Chief Minister.

questions.question: The Advocate General of a State is appointed by:
questions.explanation: Under Article 165, the Governor appoints the Advocate General of the State.

questions.question: The power of the Supreme Court to review its own judgment is under:
questions.explanation: Article 137 empowers the Supreme Court to review its own judgments.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the freedom to assemble peacefully?
questions.explanation: Article 19(1)(b) guarantees the right to assemble peacefully and without arms.

questions.question: The maximum gap between two sessions of Parliament is:
questions.explanation: Under Article 85, there cannot be more than six months between two sessions of Parliament.

questions.question: The concept of Parliamentary Privileges is mentioned in:
questions.explanation: Article 105 provides powers and privileges of Parliament and its members.

questions.question: The Union List contains how many subjects (originally)?
questions.explanation: Originally, the Union List contained 97 subjects under the Seventh Schedule.

questions.question: The Residuary Powers in India belong to:
questions.explanation: Residuary powers (subjects not mentioned in any list) are vested in the Union Parliament.

questions.question: The Oath of the President is administered by:
questions.explanation: The Chief Justice of India administers the oath to the President.

questions.question: The Proclamation of National Emergency must be approved within:
questions.explanation: The proclamation must be approved by Parliament within one month.

questions.question: Which Article deals with Disqualification of Members of Parliament?
questions.explanation: Article 102 provides grounds for disqualification of MPs.

questions.question: Which body conducts elections to State Legislatures?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission of India conducts elections for Parliament and State Legislatures.

questions.question: Which Article deals with Freedom of Trade, Commerce and Intercourse?
questions.explanation: Article 301 guarantees freedom of trade, commerce, and intercourse throughout India.

title: Constitutional Articles I
description: Special status, emergency provisions, and parliamentary powers

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Special Status of Jammu & Kashmir before its abrogation?
questions.explanation: Article 370 granted special autonomous status to Jammu & Kashmir before it was effectively abrogated in 2019.

questions.question: Which Article empowers Parliament to legislate on State List subjects during National Emergency?
questions.explanation: Article 250 allows Parliament to legislate on State List subjects during National Emergency.

questions.question: The Parliament can amend the Constitution by which majority?
questions.explanation: Article 368 requires a special majority for constitutional amendments.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Establishment of Administrative Tribunals?
questions.explanation: Article 323A provides for administrative tribunals to handle service matters.

questions.question: The Chairman of the Finance Commission is appointed by:
questions.explanation: Under Article 280, the President appoints the Finance Commission and its Chairman.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the appointment of Supreme Court Judges?
questions.explanation: Article 124 provides for the establishment and appointment of Supreme Court Judges.

questions.question: The Speaker of Lok Sabha is elected by:
questions.explanation: The Speaker is elected by the members of Lok Sabha.

questions.question: Which Article provides for the creation of All India Services?
questions.explanation: Article 312 empowers Parliament to create All India Services like IAS, IPS.

questions.question: The Constitution of India is the longest written Constitution in the world because:
options: It is based only on British system
questions.explanation: The Indian Constitution contains detailed administrative, legal, and structural provisions.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the abolition of titles?
questions.explanation: Article 18 abolishes titles except military and academic distinctions.

questions.question: The procedure for impeachment of the President is mentioned in:
questions.explanation: Article 61 lays down the procedure for impeachment of the President.

questions.question: The power to transfer High Court Judges lies with:
questions.explanation: Under Article 222, the President can transfer High Court Judges.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Power of President to grant pardon in State matters?
questions.explanation: Article 161 empowers the Governor to grant pardons in state matters.

questions.question: The National Commission for Scheduled Castes is provided under:
questions.explanation: Article 338 provides for the National Commission for Scheduled Castes.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Amendment of the Constitution?
questions.explanation: Article 368 lays down the procedure for constitutional amendments.

title: Constitutional Articles II
description: State councils, child rights, and official languages

questions.question: Which Article deals with the creation or abolition of Legislative Councils in States?
questions.explanation: Article 169 empowers Parliament to create or abolish a State Legislative Council if the concerned State Legislative Assembly passes a resolution to that effect.

questions.question: The Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha) can have a maximum strength of:
questions.explanation: As per Article 170, the maximum strength of a State Legislative Assembly is 500 members.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Right against Exploitation of children in factories?
questions.explanation: Article 24 prohibits employment of children below 14 years in factories, mines, and hazardous occupations.

questions.question: Which Article provides for the Official Language of the Union?
questions.explanation: Article 343 states that Hindi in Devanagari script shall be the official language of the Union.

questions.question: The Consolidated Fund of India is mentioned under:
questions.explanation: Article 266 provides for the Consolidated Fund of India and the States.

questions.question: Which Article provides that no money bill shall be introduced without prior recommendation of the President?
questions.explanation: Article 117 requires prior recommendation of the President for introduction of certain financial bills.

questions.question: The Election of the Vice-President is mentioned under:
questions.explanation: Article 66 provides for the election of the Vice-President by members of both Houses of Parliament.

questions.question: Which Article provides for the National Commission for Backward Classes?
questions.explanation: Article 338B (inserted by 102nd Amendment) provides constitutional status to the National Commission for Backward Classes.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Procedure in Parliament with respect to estimates”?
questions.explanation: Article 113 deals with procedure in Parliament regarding estimates (Demands for Grants).

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Annual Financial Statement”?
questions.explanation: Article 112 provides for the Annual Financial Statement, commonly known as the Budget.

questions.question: The power of Parliament to legislate for two or more States by consent is under:
questions.explanation: Article 252 allows Parliament to legislate for two or more States with their consent.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Service under the Union and the States”?
questions.explanation: Article 308 begins Part XIV dealing with services under the Union and States.

questions.question: The High Court has superintendence over subordinate courts under:
questions.explanation: Article 227 provides that High Courts have superintendence over all subordinate courts within their jurisdiction.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Oath of Members of Parliament?
questions.explanation: Article 99 requires every MP to take oath before taking their seat.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Special provisions relating to certain States”?
questions.explanation: Article 371 provides special provisions for certain States like Maharashtra, Gujarat, Nagaland, etc.

title: Judiciary and State Affairs
description: Attorney General, SC removal, and language schedules

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Attorney General for India?
questions.explanation: Article 76 provides for the appointment, qualifications, and duties of the Attorney General of India, who is the highest law officer of the Union.

questions.question: The procedure for removal of Supreme Court judges is provided under:
questions.explanation: Article 124(4) lays down the procedure for removal of Supreme Court judges through impeachment by Parliament.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Salaries of Supreme Court Judges?
questions.explanation: Article 125 provides for the salaries and allowances of Supreme Court judges.

questions.question: Which Article empowers the President to consult the Supreme Court?
questions.explanation: Article 143 gives the President the power to seek advisory opinion from the Supreme Court.

questions.question: The Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad) is a:
questions.explanation: Like Rajya Sabha, the Legislative Council is a permanent body and cannot be dissolved.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Failure of Constitutional Machinery in States”?
questions.explanation: Article 356 provides for President’s Rule in a State.

questions.question: Which Article provides for “Protection of Interests of Minorities”?
questions.explanation: Article 29 protects the cultural and educational rights of minorities.

questions.question: The Contingency Fund of India is mentioned under:
questions.explanation: Article 267 provides for the Contingency Fund of India for urgent unforeseen expenditures.

questions.question: The President addresses Parliament at the commencement of:
questions.explanation: Under Article 87, the President addresses both Houses at the commencement of the first session each year.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the Freedom to form Associations or Unions?
questions.explanation: Article 19(1)(c) guarantees the freedom to form associations or unions.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Protection of Life and Personal Liberty” interpretation expanded in Maneka Gandhi case?
questions.explanation: Article 21 has been expansively interpreted to include various rights such as right to privacy and dignity.

questions.question: The Eighth Schedule currently contains how many languages?
questions.explanation: The Eighth Schedule presently recognizes 22 official languages.

questions.question: The Rajya Sabha cannot:
questions.explanation: Rajya Sabha can only recommend changes to a Money Bill but cannot reject it.

questions.question: Which Article provides for “Grants-in-aid” to States?
questions.explanation: Article 275 provides for grants-in-aid from the Union to certain States.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Continuance of existing laws”?
questions.explanation: Article 372 provides that laws in force before the commencement of the Constitution shall continue unless altered or repealed.

title: Parliamentary Procedures
description: Joint sittings, ordinance power, and fundamental remedies

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Procedure for Joint Sitting of Parliament”?
questions.explanation: Article 108 provides for a joint sitting of both Houses of Parliament in case of a deadlock over a bill (except Money Bills and Constitutional Amendment Bills).

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Eligibility for Membership of Parliament”?
questions.explanation: Article 84 lays down qualifications required for membership of Parliament.

questions.question: Which Article provides that “No person shall be convicted of any offence except for violation of a law in force”?
questions.explanation: Article 20 protects against ex-post facto laws, double jeopardy, and self-incrimination.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Protection against Arrest and Detention”?
questions.explanation: Article 22 provides safeguards against arbitrary arrest and detention.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Official Language of a State”?
questions.explanation: Article 345 empowers State Legislatures to adopt any language as the official language of the State.

questions.question: The Governor’s power to reserve a bill for President’s consideration is under:
questions.explanation: Article 200 allows the Governor to reserve a State bill for the President’s consideration.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Special Provisions for Scheduled Areas”?
questions.explanation: Article 244 provides for administration of Scheduled Areas and Tribal Areas.

questions.question: Which Article provides for the “Council of Ministers in States”?
questions.explanation: Article 164 provides for appointment of Chief Minister and Council of Ministers in States.

questions.question: The power of the Supreme Court to grant Special Leave to Appeal is under:
questions.explanation: Article 136 gives the Supreme Court discretionary power to grant special leave to appeal.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Power of President to promulgate Ordinances during recess of Parliament”?
questions.explanation: Article 123 empowers the President to issue Ordinances when Parliament is not in session.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Provisions as to Financial Bills”?
questions.explanation: Article 117 provides special provisions for Financial Bills.

questions.question: Which Article provides for the “Right to Move the Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights”?
questions.explanation: Article 32 guarantees the right to constitutional remedies.

questions.question: The power of Parliament to legislate for implementing international treaties is under:
questions.explanation: Article 253 empowers Parliament to legislate for implementing international agreements.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Freedom of Conscience”?
questions.explanation: Article 25 guarantees freedom of conscience and free profession, practice, and propagation of religion.

questions.question: Which Article provides for “Public Service Commissions for the Union and the States”?
questions.explanation: Article 315 provides for the establishment of Public Service Commissions.

title: Parliamentary Mechanics
description: Sessions, Money Bills, and disqualification rules

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Duration of Houses of Parliament”?
questions.explanation: Article 83 provides that Rajya Sabha is a permanent body and Lok Sabha has a normal term of five years unless dissolved earlier.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Sessions of Parliament, Prorogation and Dissolution”?
questions.explanation: Article 85 empowers the President to summon, prorogue, and dissolve the Lok Sabha.

questions.question: Which Article provides for the “Special Address by the President”?
questions.explanation: Article 87 mandates the President’s address to both Houses at the commencement of the first session each year and after general elections.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Right of Ministers and Attorney General to speak in Parliament”?
questions.explanation: Article 88 allows Ministers and the Attorney General to participate in proceedings of Parliament, though they may not vote if not members.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Vacancy of Seats in Parliament”?
questions.explanation: Article 101 deals with vacation of seats and disqualifications of members.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Decision on Questions as to Disqualifications of Members”?
questions.explanation: Article 103 states that the President decides questions of disqualification after consulting the Election Commission.

questions.question: Which Article defines a “Money Bill”?
questions.explanation: Article 110 defines what constitutes a Money Bill.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Appropriation Bills”?
questions.explanation: Article 114 provides for Appropriation Bills after Demands for Grants are voted.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Supplementary, Additional or Excess Grants”?
questions.explanation: Article 115 deals with supplementary or additional grants required during a financial year.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Vote on Account, Vote of Credit and Exceptional Grants”?
questions.explanation: Article 116 provides for temporary financial arrangements like vote on account.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Rules of Procedure of Parliament”?
questions.explanation: Article 118 empowers each House to make rules for regulating its procedure.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Language to be used in Parliament”?
questions.explanation: Article 120 provides that proceedings in Parliament shall be conducted in Hindi or English.

questions.question: Which Article restricts discussion on conduct of Supreme Court judges in Parliament?
questions.explanation: Article 121 prohibits discussion on conduct of judges except during impeachment proceedings.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Courts not to inquire into proceedings of Parliament”?
questions.explanation: Article 122 bars courts from questioning parliamentary proceedings on procedural grounds.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Establishment of the Supreme Court”?
questions.explanation: Article 124 provides for the establishment and constitution of the Supreme Court of India.

title: Supreme Court & High Court I
description: Judicial jurisdiction, binding law, and court appointments

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Appointment of Acting Chief Justice of India”?
questions.explanation: Article 126 provides that when the office of Chief Justice of India is vacant or the CJI is unable to perform duties, the President appoints an Acting Chief Justice.

questions.question: Which Article allows appointment of “Ad hoc Judges” in the Supreme Court?
questions.explanation: Article 127 provides for appointment of ad hoc judges in the Supreme Court when there is a lack of quorum.

questions.question: The Supreme Court is a “Court of Record” under:
questions.explanation: Article 129 declares the Supreme Court as a Court of Record with power to punish for contempt.

questions.question: The Seat of the Supreme Court is determined under:
questions.explanation: Article 130 states that the Supreme Court shall sit in Delhi or at such other place as the Chief Justice may appoint with the President’s approval.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Appellate Jurisdiction of Supreme Court in Constitutional Matters”?
questions.explanation: Article 132 provides for appellate jurisdiction in constitutional cases.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Appellate Jurisdiction in Civil Matters”?
questions.explanation: Article 133 provides for appeals to the Supreme Court in civil cases.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Appellate Jurisdiction in Criminal Matters”?
questions.explanation: Article 134 provides appellate jurisdiction in criminal matters.

questions.question: Which Article states that “Law declared by the Supreme Court is binding on all courts”?
questions.explanation: Article 141 states that the law declared by the Supreme Court shall be binding on all courts within India.

questions.question: Which Article empowers the Supreme Court to pass orders for “Complete Justice”?
questions.explanation: Article 142 empowers the Supreme Court to pass any order necessary for complete justice.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Enlargement of Jurisdiction of the Supreme Court”?
questions.explanation: Article 138 allows Parliament to enlarge the jurisdiction of the Supreme Court.

questions.question: Which Article provides for “Transfer of certain cases to Supreme Court”?
questions.explanation: Article 139A allows transfer of cases from High Courts to the Supreme Court.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Ancillary powers of Supreme Court”?
questions.explanation: Article 140 empowers Parliament to confer supplementary powers on the Supreme Court.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Establishment of High Courts for States”?
questions.explanation: Article 214 states that each State shall have a High Court.

questions.question: High Courts are Courts of Record under:
questions.explanation: Article 215 declares every High Court to be a Court of Record.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Appointment and Conditions of High Court Judges”?
questions.explanation: Article 217 deals with appointment, tenure, and qualifications of High Court judges.

title: High Court & Subordinate Courts
description: Judicial oaths, transfers, and district judge appointments

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Oath or Affirmation by Judges of High Court”?
questions.explanation: Article 219 provides that every High Court judge shall take an oath before entering office.

questions.question: Which Article restricts practice after being a Permanent Judge of a High Court?
questions.explanation: Article 220 prohibits permanent High Court judges from pleading or acting in courts except the Supreme Court and other High Courts.

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Salaries of High Court Judges”?
questions.explanation: Article 221 provides for salaries and allowances of High Court judges.

questions.question: Which Article provides for the “Transfer of High Court Judges”?
questions.explanation: Article 222 empowers the President to transfer a judge from one High Court to another.

questions.question: Which Article provides for “Appointment of Acting Chief Justice of High Court”?
questions.explanation: Article 223 provides for appointment of an Acting Chief Justice in case of vacancy.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Appointment of Additional and Acting Judges”?
questions.explanation: Article 224 allows appointment of additional and acting judges in High Courts.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Jurisdiction of existing High Courts”?
questions.explanation: Article 225 preserves the jurisdiction of existing High Courts.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Transfer of certain cases to High Court”?
questions.explanation: Article 228 provides for transfer of certain constitutional cases to High Court.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Officers and Servants of High Court”?
questions.explanation: Article 229 deals with appointment and conditions of service of High Court staff.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Extension of Jurisdiction of High Courts to Union Territories”?
questions.explanation: Article 230 allows Parliament to extend High Court jurisdiction to Union Territories.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Establishment of a common High Court for two or more States”?
questions.explanation: Article 231 provides for establishment of a common High Court for multiple States.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Appointment of District Judges”?
questions.explanation: Article 233 provides for appointment of District Judges by the Governor in consultation with High Court.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Recruitment of persons other than District Judges”?
questions.explanation: Article 234 deals with recruitment to judicial service other than District Judges.

questions.question: Which Article provides High Court control over subordinate courts?
questions.explanation: Article 235 gives High Courts control over district and subordinate courts.

questions.question: Which Article defines “District Judge”?
questions.explanation: Article 236 defines the term “District Judge” for constitutional purposes.

title: DPSP and Public Services
description: Judicial services, DPSP articles, and CAG duties

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Creation of All India Judicial Service”?
questions.explanation: Article 312 empowers Parliament to create All India Services, including the All India Judicial Service, if Rajya Sabha passes a resolution by special majority.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Public Service Commissions – Functions”?
questions.explanation: Article 320 specifies the functions of UPSC and State PSCs, including conducting examinations and advising on service matters.

questions.question: The term of office of a UPSC member is:
questions.explanation: A UPSC member holds office for 6 years or until attaining the age of 65 years, whichever is earlier.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Special Officer for Linguistic Minorities”?
questions.explanation: Article 350B provides for the appointment of a Special Officer for Linguistic Minorities.

questions.question: Which Article directs the State to provide free and compulsory education for children?
questions.explanation: Originally, Article 45 (DPSP) directed the State to provide free and compulsory education. Later, Article 21A made it a Fundamental Right.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Promotion of educational and economic interests of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes”?
questions.explanation: Article 46 directs the State to promote educational and economic interests of SCs and STs.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Uniform Civil Code”?
questions.explanation: Article 44 (DPSP) directs the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code for citizens.

questions.question: Which Article directs the State to organize village panchayats?
questions.explanation: Article 40 directs the State to organize village panchayats.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Equal justice and free legal aid”?
questions.explanation: Article 39A directs the State to ensure equal justice and free legal aid.

questions.question: Which Article directs the State to raise the level of nutrition and public health?
questions.explanation: Article 47 makes improvement of public health a primary duty of the State.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Organization of agriculture and animal husbandry”?
questions.explanation: Article 48 directs the State to organize agriculture and animal husbandry scientifically.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Protection of monuments and places of national importance”?
questions.explanation: Article 49 directs the State to protect monuments and places of national importance.

questions.question: Which Article provides for “Separation of Judiciary from Executive”?
questions.explanation: Article 50 directs the State to separate the judiciary from the executive in public services.

questions.question: Which Article promotes “International Peace and Security”?
questions.explanation: Article 51 directs the State to promote international peace and security.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Powers and Functions of the Comptroller and Auditor General”?
questions.explanation: Article 149 defines the duties and powers of the CAG.

title: Executive & State Legislatures
description: PM duties, Governor addresses, and legislature composition

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Extent of Executive Power of the Union”?
questions.explanation: Article 73 defines the extent of the executive power of the Union, which extends to matters on which Parliament has authority to legislate.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Conduct of Business of the Government of India”?
questions.explanation: Article 77 provides that executive actions of the Government of India shall be expressed in the name of the President.

questions.question: Which Article lays down the “Duties of the Prime Minister”?
questions.explanation: Article 78 specifies the duties of the Prime Minister, including keeping the President informed.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Extent of Executive Power of the State”?
questions.explanation: Article 162 defines the extent of executive power of a State.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Conduct of Business of the Government of a State”?
questions.explanation: Article 166 provides that executive actions of a State shall be expressed in the name of the Governor.

questions.question: Which Article lays down the “Duties of the Chief Minister”?
questions.explanation: Article 167 outlines duties of the Chief Minister in relation to the Governor.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Composition of State Legislature”?
questions.explanation: Article 168 provides for the constitution of Legislatures in States.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Composition of Legislative Assembly”?
questions.explanation: Article 170 deals with composition and strength of the Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Composition of Legislative Council”?
questions.explanation: Article 171 provides for composition of Legislative Councils.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Duration of State Legislatures”?
questions.explanation: Article 172 states that the Legislative Assembly has a term of five years unless dissolved earlier.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Qualifications for Membership of State Legislature”?
questions.explanation: Article 173 lays down qualifications for membership of State Legislature.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Sessions of State Legislature”?
questions.explanation: Article 174 empowers the Governor to summon, prorogue and dissolve the State Legislature.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Right of Governor to Address and Send Messages”?
questions.explanation: Article 175 gives the Governor the right to address the Legislature and send messages.

questions.question: Which Article provides for “Special Address by Governor”?
questions.explanation: Article 176 requires the Governor to address the State Legislature at the commencement of the first session each year.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Rights of Ministers and Advocate-General in State Legislature”?
questions.explanation: Article 177 allows Ministers and Advocate-General to participate in State Legislature proceedings.

title: State Legislature Officers
description: Speaker, Chairman, and legislative disqualifications

questions.question: Which Article deals with the “Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Legislative Assembly”?
questions.explanation: Article 178 provides for the election of Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Legislative Assembly.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Vacation and Resignation of Speaker and Deputy Speaker”?
questions.explanation: Article 179 lays down conditions for vacation, resignation, and removal of Speaker and Deputy Speaker.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Power of Deputy Speaker to Perform Duties of Speaker”?
questions.explanation: Article 180 allows Deputy Speaker to perform the duties when the office of Speaker is vacant.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Speaker not to Preside while Resolution for Removal is Under Consideration”?
questions.explanation: Article 181 states that the Speaker shall not preside when a resolution for removal is under consideration.

questions.question: Which Article provides for “Chairman and Deputy Chairman of Legislative Council”?
questions.explanation: Article 182 deals with election of Chairman and Deputy Chairman of Legislative Council.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Vacation and Resignation of Chairman and Deputy Chairman”?
questions.explanation: Article 183 provides provisions for vacation, resignation, and removal of Chairman and Deputy Chairman.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Power of Deputy Chairman to Perform Duties of Chairman”?
questions.explanation: Article 184 empowers Deputy Chairman to perform Chairman’s duties in his absence.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Chairman not to Preside while Resolution for Removal is Under Consideration”?
questions.explanation: Article 185 prevents Chairman from presiding when removal motion is being discussed.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Salaries and Allowances of Speaker and Deputy Speaker”?
questions.explanation: Article 186 provides for salaries and allowances of presiding officers of State Legislature.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Secretariat of State Legislature”?
questions.explanation: Article 187 provides for separate secretariat staff for State Legislature.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Oath or Affirmation by Members of State Legislature”?
questions.explanation: Article 188 requires members to take oath before taking their seats.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Voting in Houses and Power of Houses to Act”?
questions.explanation: Article 189 provides rules regarding voting and quorum in State Legislature.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Vacation of Seats in State Legislature”?
questions.explanation: Article 190 deals with vacation of seats of members of State Legislature.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Disqualifications for Membership of State Legislature”?
questions.explanation: Article 191 specifies grounds of disqualification for members.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Decision on Questions as to Disqualifications of Members of State Legislature”?
questions.explanation: Article 192 states that the Governor decides questions of disqualification after consulting the Election Commission.

title: State Financial Procedures
description: State money bills, budgets, and legislative privileges

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Penalty for sitting and voting before making oath”?
questions.explanation: Article 193 provides penalty for members who sit or vote before taking oath or when disqualified.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Powers, Privileges and Immunities of State Legislatures”?
questions.explanation: Article 194 grants powers and privileges to State Legislatures similar to Parliament under Article 105.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Salaries and Allowances of Members of State Legislature”?
questions.explanation: Article 195 provides for salaries and allowances of members of State Legislature.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Provisions as to Introduction and Passing of Bills in State Legislature”?
questions.explanation: Article 196 lays down general procedure for introduction and passing of Bills in State Legislature.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Restriction on Powers of Legislative Council as to Bills other than Money Bills”?
questions.explanation: Article 197 limits the powers of the Legislative Council in respect of ordinary Bills.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Special Procedure in respect of Money Bills in States”?
questions.explanation: Article 198 provides special procedure for Money Bills in State Legislatures.

questions.question: Which Article defines a “Money Bill” in State Legislature?
questions.explanation: Article 199 defines what constitutes a Money Bill at the State level.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Assent to Bills by Governor”?
questions.explanation: Article 200 provides options available to the Governor regarding assent to Bills.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Bills Reserved for Consideration of the President”?
questions.explanation: Article 201 deals with Bills reserved by the Governor for President’s consideration.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Annual Financial Statement in States”?
questions.explanation: Article 202 provides for the State Budget (Annual Financial Statement).

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Procedure in Legislature with respect to Estimates”?
questions.explanation: Article 203 deals with Demands for Grants in State Legislature.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Appropriation Bills in States”?
questions.explanation: Article 204 provides for Appropriation Bills after grants are voted.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Supplementary, Additional or Excess Grants in States”?
questions.explanation: Article 205 provides for supplementary or excess grants in State finances.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Vote on Account, Vote of Credit and Exceptional Grants in States”?
questions.explanation: Article 206 allows temporary financial arrangements in State Legislature.

questions.question: Which Article deals with “Special Provisions as to Financial Bills in States”?
questions.explanation: Article 207 provides special provisions regarding Financial Bills in State Legislatures.
